Notice Of Motion And Motion For Separate Trials To Be Heard By The Same Jury (Bifurcation) On Plaintiffs Claims For Punitive Damages And Order That Evidence Of Profit And Financial Condition Is Inadmissible During The First Trial
Off Calendar Jan-13-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER's Motion For Separate Trials To Be Heard By The Same Jury (Bifurcation) On Plaintiffs Claims For Punitive Damages And Order That Evidence Of Profit And Financial Condition Is Inadmissible During The First Trial.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' motion for separate trials is denied without prejudice; it is our court's practice to defer to the trial judge on bifurcation motions.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
